name: Benton County State Bank
description_short: Benton County State Bank is a community bank established in 1869 offering deposit accounts, loans, and financial services to individuals and businesses.
description_long: Benton County State Bank has operated as a community financial institution since 1869, building its reputation on long-term customer relationships and localized service. The bank serves both individual and commercial customers through a single physical location with specific operating hours (Monday-Thursday 8:30am-3:00pm, Friday 8:30am-5:00pm). Their service model emphasizes understanding customer needs and priorities to deliver tailored financial solutions rather than a broad national presence. The bank maintains an active Community Reinvestment Act (CRA) program, indicating commitment to serving their local assessment area. Their long operational history suggests stability and established roots within their community. However, as a small community bank with limited branch presence and restricted hours, they may not suit customers seeking 24/7 access or extensive branch networks. The institution appears to serve primarily local customers in their geographic area rather than offering online-first or national banking services. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Established community bank with 155+ years of operating history (since 1869)
Offers notary services to both customers and non-customers
Comprehensive loan products including agricultural, business, and consumer loans
Night depository service for after-hours deposits
Safe deposit box services for document and valuables storage
Full range of deposit products including checking, savings, CDs, and IRAs
Account reconciliation and research services available
cons: Limited branch presence and restricted operating hours (closes at 3pm most days)
No mention of extended digital banking capabilities or mobile app features
Notary services charge fees for non-customers, creating potential barriers
Limited information available about loan terms, rates, or eligibility requirements on website
No indication of online account opening or remote service capabilities
best_for: Local customers in Benton County, Iowa seeking personalized community banking
Agricultural borrowers requiring specialized farm lending expertise
Small business owners needing relationship-based commercial banking
diagnosis: Best for established local customers in Benton County, Iowa who value personal relationships and can work within business hours. Main caveat: This is a small community bank with limited hours and no apparent digital-first capabilities, making it unsuitable for customers seeking 24/7 access or those outside the immediate service area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
